content.claim: Between 2004 and 2008, the number of villages independently maintaining primary schools was halved due to legislative/fiscal policy shifts in state incentive structures, demonstrating that closure patterns reflect political choices rather than demographic inevitability.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Historical precedent does not guarantee that the same fiscal levers remain available or unchanged in 2026 tankerület system
Does not isolate demographic versus policy contribution to closures
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
